templates.text: It is recommended that a professional drain survey / CCTV inspection be carried out to assess the condition of the drainage system and identify any defects or rodent entry points.
Line jetting is recommended to clear grease, scale or debris restricting flow in the affected drain run.
Drain unblocking is recommended to restore discharge where restriction or blockage has been identified.
Installation of a 4 inch (100 mm) non-return rat flap is recommended to reduce the risk of rodents entering the property from the sewer system.
Installation of a 6 inch (150 mm) non-return rat flap is recommended on the foul drain connection serving the property.
Installation of a 9 inch (225 mm) non-return rat flap is recommended for the larger diameter drain connection identified on site.
Installation of a 12 inch (300 mm) non-return rat flap is recommended for the main drainage outlet / commercial sewer connection.
Drain benching repairs are recommended to renew the chamber fillet and direct flow correctly into the channel, reducing pooling and odours.
Localised drain patch repair is recommended where structural defects or open joints have been identified.
Manhole benching or chamber repairs are recommended to improve flow and reduce odours / harbourage in the chamber.
Gully cleaning is recommended to remove debris and restore surface water discharge.
Pipe sealing / sealing of redundant connections is recommended to close identified entry points in the drainage system.
Minor proofing works are recommended to seal identified gaps, holes and potential rodent entry points noted during this visit.
Installation of brush strip / door brush is recommended to reduce pest entry at the base of the door(s) noted.
Bristle strip proofing is recommended to close gaps at door thresholds and restrict pest ingress.
Door brush proofing is recommended to the affected door(s) to minimise gap access at the threshold.
Sealant works are recommended to close small gaps and cracks identified during inspection.
Proofing mesh is recommended to protect vented openings / voids and prevent rodent access.
Wire wool or copper mesh exclusion is recommended to block small holes and pipe entry points.
Replacement or securing of drain covers / grates is recommended where covers are damaged, missing or allow access.
No specific recommendations were noted at the time of this visit.
Improved hygiene and housekeeping are recommended, including secure waste storage and removal of harbourage and food sources.
It is recommended that the current monitoring / baiting programme continue and be reviewed on the next scheduled visit.
Increased monitoring is recommended in high-risk areas until activity is brought under control.
Proofing of gaps around pipe penetrations, doors and windows is recommended to reduce insect ingress.
It is recommended that an additional fly control unit is installed in the affected area to improve coverage and monitoring of flying insect activity.
Fly screens or screen repairs are recommended to windows / doors where flying insects are entering.
Additional insect monitoring (e.g. glue boards / monitors) is recommended in affected areas.
It is recommended that the treated nest area is left undisturbed and monitored; further advice can be provided if activity persists.
A full attic clean is recommended, including hoovering of evident droppings, removal of contaminated insulation where required, and application of antibacterial treatment to affected surfaces.
Hoovering / removal of evident droppings in the attic space is recommended to reduce contamination and pest harbourage.
Removal of contaminated attic insulation is recommended where droppings, nesting material or urine staining has been identified.
Application of antibacterial spray / sanitation treatment to affected attic surfaces is recommended following droppings removal and cleaning.
